subject: SOC2 Vendor Security Questionnaire — Response needed
body_plain:
Hi Lance,

As part of our annual vendor review, we need you to complete the attached SOC2 security questionnaire for Eragon. The deadline is June 3, 2026. This covers access controls, encryption, and incident response procedures.

Please respond to this email with the completed form.

Thanks,
Sarah Chen
Compliance, PartnerIO
snippet: We need you to complete the SOC2 security questionnaire for Eragon. Deadline is June 3, 2026.

subject: Scheduling — GP Partner intro call with you
body_plain:
Lance,

Our General Partner, Marcus Webb, would like to schedule a 30-minute intro call with you next week. Could you share your availability for May 28–30? We're particularly interested in learning about Eragon's roadmap.

Best,
Emily Zhao
Partner at Tier1 VC
snippet: Marcus Webb would like to schedule a 30-minute intro call with you next week. Share your availability for May 28-30.

subject: Suspicious password reset attempt on your account
body_plain:
Hi Lance,

We detected an unusual password reset request for your Eragon internal account originating from IP 185.220.101.47 (Tor exit node, Netherlands). If this was not you, please immediately secure your account and contact security@eragon.ai.

Eragon Security Team
snippet: We detected an unusual password reset request from IP 185.220.101.47 (Tor exit node). If this was not you, secure your account.

subject: Re: Suspicious password reset attempt — second attempt detected
body_plain:
Hi Lance,

This is a follow-up alert. A second password reset attempt was made on your account 14 minutes after the first, from a different IP: 91.108.4.200 (also flagged). Your account has been temporarily locked as a precaution. Please contact security@eragon.ai urgently.

Eragon Security Team
snippet: Second password reset attempt detected from 91.108.4.200. Your account has been temporarily locked. Contact security urgently.

subject: 5 Figma tips to speed up your workflow
body_plain:
Work smarter in Figma:

1. Use components everywhere — Stop copy-pasting, start using components.
2. Master auto-layout — Responsive designs in seconds.
3. Prototype faster with variables — One design, many states.
4. Collaborate with Dev Mode — Hand off without the handoff friction.
5. Try Figma AI — Generate UI from a text prompt.

Learn more at figma.com/resources
snippet: Work smarter in Figma. 5 tips: use components everywhere, master auto-layout, prototype with variables, Dev Mode, Figma AI.

subject: Quick question about Eragon's outbound stack
body_plain:
Hi Lance,

I came across Eragon and was impressed by what you're building. I wanted to ask — are you happy with your current outbound sales stack? We help AI-native companies like yours close 3x more deals with less SDR headcount.

Worth a 15-minute call?

Jake Morrison
Account Executive, SalesLoft

Rachel Nguyen
